title: haplopappus diplopappus subsp. diplopappus
intro: Haplopappus diplopappus subsp. diplopappus, also known as the Rocky Mountain goldenrod, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Haplopappus diplopappus subsp. diplopappus is typically prepared as an infusion or tincture for herbal remedies, and it can also be used as a topical application for skin issues and wounds.
